# @vllnt/convex-experiments

> Deterministic A/B experimentation — sticky variant assignment and deduped exposure tracking, as a Convex component

`@vllnt/convex-experiments` is a publishable Convex component. It targets `convex@^1.41.0`. It stores
four sandboxed tables — `experiments` (the definition: weighted `variants`, lifecycle `status`,
`salt`, unique per `(scope, key)`), `assignments` (a subject's sticky variant, persisted on first
`assign`), `exposures` (a deduped per-subject row), and `variantTallies` (maintained per-variant
`assigned`/`subjects`/`exposures` counts). A subject's variant is a pure function of
`(scope, salt, subjectRef)` — FNV-1a hashed onto the cumulative-weight line, `scope` folded in so the
same subject buckets independently per scope — sticky and weight-respecting even before persistence;
`assign` records it, later calls replay it, and `peek` returns it read-only (no write) for SSR. `salt`
and `variants` are immutable once a subject is assigned (`EXPERIMENT_LOCKED`); re-randomize by defining
a new key. Enrollment is gated on `running` (`draft`/`stopped`/absent return `{ variant: null }`).
`logExposure` dedups exposures and advances the tallies; `results` reads them in O(variants), returning
`assigned`/`subjects`/`exposures`/`weight` per variant (enough for a sample-ratio-mismatch check).
`forgetSubject` erases one subject (GDPR), `deleteExperiment` cascades, `listExperiments` discovers.
Time is server-sourced. The component assigns + counts exposure; the host owns release control, the
experiment/variant meaning, the opaque `subjectRef`, statistical significance, and the outcome metric
(measured in the host's own tables, joined on the variant).
